claim_text: 53-project scan is deep enough to drive stable eval decisions
counterargument: Breadth does not guarantee representativeness; key enterprise/private stacks may be under-covered
boundary_condition_not_hold: Organization relies on proprietary observability stack or strict on-prem tools
recommendation_for_c: Add coverage disclaimer and missing-stack inventory

claim_text: Offline-to-online closed loop is generally achievable
counterargument: Loop fails without dedicated dataset ownership and labeling discipline
boundary_condition_not_hold: No assigned eval owner and no incident-to-dataset process
recommendation_for_c: Require owner + SLA before calling it closed loop

claim_text: PR gate thresholds are broadly reusable
counterargument: Fixed thresholds can misfit diverse task classes and languages
boundary_condition_not_hold: Multilingual or long-context workflows with different baseline variance

claim_text: Nightly shadow_prod regression is sufficient drift defense
counterargument: Nightly cadence can miss fast regressions after urgent changes
boundary_condition_not_hold: High-frequency deployment teams with multiple daily releases
recommendation_for_c: Add post-deploy immediate smoke eval tier

claim_text: Prompt injection pass-rate >=0.95 is a robust default
counterargument: Single pass-rate metric can be gamed and hide attack class gaps
boundary_condition_not_hold: Attack suite lacks indirect injection and tool-chain pivot cases

claim_text: 组合A (langfuse+deepeval+ragas+promptfoo+guardrails+litellm) is default open-source route
counterargument: Combination may create overlapping telemetry and evaluator semantics causing noisy decisions
boundary_condition_not_hold: No normalized event schema across tools
recommendation_for_c: Define canonical schema before multi-tool stack

counterargument: Rollback SLO can be unrealistic when dependency graph spans model, gateway, and policy layers
boundary_condition_not_hold: Cross-team approvals and manual release freezes required
recommendation_for_c: Set tiered rollback target by incident class

claim_text: Error-rate and success-rate triggers are enough for rollback
counterargument: Quality degradation can occur without immediate error spike
boundary_condition_not_hold: Hallucination/semantic drift in apparently successful responses
recommendation_for_c: Add semantic quality trigger to rollback criteria

claim_text: Threat chain model is practical and complete for OwlClaw-like infra
counterargument: Chain omits model supply and data labeling poisoning specifics in detail
boundary_condition_not_hold: Organization trains/fine-tunes or ingests external untrusted datasets
recommendation_for_c: Extend chain with model/data supply attack nodes

claim_text: Policy engine + auth graph + sandbox should be treated as one stack
counterargument: Unified stack can be overkill for read-only low-risk tool environments
boundary_condition_not_hold: Read-only tools with no write/exec capability
recommendation_for_c: Support minimal-security profile for low-risk workloads

claim_text: Output safety needs structure+privacy+exec risk gates always
counterargument: Some outputs are non-executable and low-risk; full chain may hurt throughput
boundary_condition_not_hold: Pure QA chat endpoints with no downstream action

claim_text: Unauthorized tool call success rate must be zero in staging
counterargument: Zero target can hide detection blind spots if attack suite is narrow
boundary_condition_not_hold: Staging suite lacks emergent tool composition attacks
recommendation_for_c: Pair KPI with adversarial coverage score

counterargument: Emergency hotfix channels may require temporary bypass if governance supports exception
boundary_condition_not_hold: Critical outage with signed pipeline outage
recommendation_for_c: Introduce time-bound exception policy and backfill audit

claim_text: Archived repos should only be baseline corpora, never dependencies
counterargument: Some archived references may still be valid if forked and internally maintained
boundary_condition_not_hold: Internal maintained fork with pinned security patches
recommendation_for_c: Allow internal-fork exception with security attestation

claim_text: Week-by-week 90-day rollout sequence is generally valid
counterargument: Sequencing assumes stable staffing and no major incident interruptions
boundary_condition_not_hold: Resource contention or parallel compliance deadlines

claim_text: Top30 scoring dimensions are enough for adoption decisions
counterargument: Current score omits migration debt and organizational readiness
boundary_condition_not_hold: Team lacks in-house expertise for chosen tools
recommendation_for_c: Add org-readiness and migration-cost dimensions before ranking

claim_text: NIST/CISA 403 can be tolerated with alternate sources
counterargument: Lack of primary-source verification may weaken audit defensibility
boundary_condition_not_hold: Regulated audits requiring direct framework traceability
recommendation_for_c: Track formal evidence debt with due date

claim_text: One primary control-plane (Langfuse or Opik) should be selected
counterargument: Single-plane strategy may create vendor concentration and migration risk
boundary_condition_not_hold: Strict anti-lock-in policy or sovereign deployment constraints
recommendation_for_c: Adopt portable telemetry schema and dual-vendor exit plan
